businessRules: The origin airport of a flight must be different from its destination airport.
At most one baggage belt is assigned to a flight, at the destination airport.
Every flight has a crew composed of one pilot, one co-pilot, and one or more cabin crew members; a crew member works on many flights over time.
A city can have multiple airports.
An airplane can make many flights.
A passenger books at most one seat per flight.
A flight's status changes over time (e.g., scheduled, boarding, departed) and every status change is recorded with its date and time.
